pi_items.source: hardcoded in task.json; anatomically verified via PMC9616175

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the AMOS 2022 dataset but defaults to synthetic data generation (a NIfTI volume with a cylindrical aorta) if the 11GB download fails. The evaluation requires a minimum of 8 control points and a curve length of at least 100 mm. Anatomical literature (PMC9616175) confirms the average abdominal aorta length is approximately 130 mm, supporting the 100 mm threshold. The resulting CPR must be at least 256 pixels in dimension and 50 KB in size, with a centerline tolerance of 10 mm.
